title: ferray-ufunc: gcd/lcm use Float trait — should work on actual integers
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

gcd() and lcm() in arithmetic.rs are bounded on T: Element + Float, which means they only work on f32/f64, not on actual integer types (i32, u64, etc.). They even check for NaN. NumPy's gcd/lcm work on integer types directly and don't produce NaN. These should have separate integer implementations using the Euclidean algorithm on integer types, plus the existing float version for compatibility.
